skillName: Find the median from a set of numbers (incl midpoint using a calculator)
misconceptionSummary: Students pick the middle-positioned value from the original unsorted list rather than sorting first.

steps.narration: Before you can find the middle, you must sort. The order in which values are given to you is irrelevant — only the sorted order matters.
Five values means the third one is in the middle — two on either side. Cross off from both ends simultaneously until one remains.
With an even number of values there is no single middle. Take the two values that share the middle and find their average.

visuals.label: Sort first — then find the middle position

steps.narration: Sort, find the middle position, read off the value. That is the complete method every time.

misconceptionStrip.text: Common mistake: picking the middle value from the unsorted list. Always sort first — the original order is meaningless for finding the median.
misconceptionStrip.audioNarration: If you haven't sorted the list, you cannot find the median. Sorting is not optional — it is step one.
